feed.title: HUMOR - International Journal of Humor Research
feed.subtitle: Latest articles from HUMOR - International Journal of Humor Research (De Gruyter)

entry.title: Introduction to the special issue: humor, trauma and healing
entry.summary: Authors: Margherita Dore DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/humor-2026-0016 Pages: Page range: 179-185

entry.title: Out-law Stand-up:Testimonial Humorin contemporary women’s stand-up comedy
entry.summary: Authors: Muge Yuce DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/humor-2024-0104 Pages: Page range: 187-211

entry.title: Humor and Dalit trauma: life narratives from Hindi literature and stand-up comedy
entry.summary: Authors: Fabio Mangraviti DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/humor-2025-0091 Pages: Page range: 213-236

entry.title: Parody and disengagement from stigma: mental illness inCrazy Ex-Girlfriend
entry.summary: Authors: Giovanni Raffa DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/humor-2024-0110 Pages: Page range: 237-253

entry.title: Trauma, postfeminist humor and healing
entry.summary: Authors: Margherita Dore DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/humor-2025-0069 Pages: Page range: 255-270

entry.title: The psychological role of “Auschwitz jokes”: humor as a coping strategy for the negative psychological consequences of traumatic historical exposure
entry.summary: Authors: Karolina Marcinkowska, Dominik Puchała, Mikołaj Winiewski, Michał Bilewicz DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/humor-2025-0029 Pages: Page range: 271-288
